{{日本の氏族
|家名 = Inaba no Kuni no Miyatsuko
|家紋 = <!-- 家紋の画像 -->
|家紋名称 =
|本姓 = {{ill|Inaba clan|ja|因幡氏}} or {{ill|Ifukube clan|ja|伊福部氏}}
|家祖 = {{ill|Hikotatsuhiko-no-Mikoto|ja|彦多都彦命}} or {{ill|Ikowashihiko-no-Sukune|ja|伊其和斯彦宿祢}}
|種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} or {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} ({{ill|Tenjin|ja|天神}})
|出身地 = {{ill|Ōyamato Province|ja|大倭国}}
|根拠地 = Inaba Province (later {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}})
|人物 = See [[#People|People]]
|支流 = {{ill|Tajima no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造}}, {{ill|Ho no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|穂国造}} or {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}}, {{ill|Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|久自国造}}, {{ill|Suruga no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|珠流河国造}}, {{ill|Tōtsuōmi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|遠淡海国造}}, {{ill|Kunu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|久努国造}}, {{ill|Kazahaya no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|風速国造}}, {{ill|Ochi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造}}, etc.
}}
'''Inaba no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''inaba no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''inaba kokuzō'') was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled the region that later became {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} ('''[[#Territory|Inaba Province]]''').

== Overview ==
=== Name ===
The "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' writes it as '''Inaba no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.

=== Ancestry ===
*The "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' transmits that during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, '''Hikotatsuhiko-no-Mikoto''', son of '''{{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto|ja|彦坐王}}''', was appointed kuni no miyatsuko.
*The ''Ifukube-shi Keizu'' transmits that '''Ikowashihiko-no-Sukune''', 8th-generation descendant of '''{{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|lt=Kushitama-no-kami-Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=櫛玉神饒速日命}}''', was appointed kuni no miyatsuko during the reign of Emperor Seimu.

=== Clan ===
'''{{ill|Inaba clan|ja|因幡氏}}''' (''inaba-uji''; {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} of {{ill|Miyatsuko|ja|造}}), granted '''Inaba-no-Miyatsuko''' in {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 2. They later took the kabane '''Inaba-no-Sukune'''. Among the kuni no miyatsuko family is also the '''{{ill|Ifukube clan|ja|伊福部氏}}''' (''ifukube-uji''; kabane of {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}). Regarding their genealogy, there is a tradition of {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}} descent from Hikoimasu-no-Mikoto, and a tradition of {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別}} descent from Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto.

The proper noun "'''Inaba'''" (稲葉, 因幡, 印旛, 印葉, 稲羽) appears in many places, including the '''Inaba no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' of the {{ill|San'indō|ja|山陰道}}, '''Inaba-gō''' and '''{{ill|Mount Inaba (Tottori)|ja|稲葉山 (鳥取県)|lt=Mount Inaba|lt_ja=稲葉山}}''' of the same province's {{ill|Hōmi District|ja|法美郡}}; '''Inaba''' in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|ja|天理市}} of {{ill|Yamato Province|ja|大和国}}; '''{{ill|Mount Inaba (Gifu)|ja|金華山 (岐阜県)|lt=Mount Inaba|lt_ja=稲葉山}}''' in {{ill|Atsumi District|ja|厚見郡}} of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}} (the central territory of the {{ill|Minogo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|三野後国造}}, where the Engishiki-listed {{ill|Mononobe Shrine|ja|物部神社}} is also located); the '''Inba''' figure in "{{ill|Tenson Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|lt=Tenson Hongi|lt_ja=天孫本紀}}" (said to be the grandson of Takemorozumi-no-Mikoto); and '''Imba-no-Sukune''', ancestor of the {{ill|Kunu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|久努国造}} in the "Kokuzō Hongi" (said to be grandson of {{ill|Ikagashikoo-no-Mikoto|ja|伊香色雄|lt=Ikagashikoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=伊香色男命}}), and others<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keihu/monobekz/monobek1.htm Examination of the Mononobe Clan Genealogy in 'Tenson Hongi']," ''Koju Kinobōma'', 2008.</ref>.

== Base ==
=== Territory ===
The territory of the kuni no miyatsuko was the region then called '''Inaba Province''', corresponding to the later {{ill|ritsuryō|ja|律令制}} '''{{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}}''', equivalent to present-day {{ill|Kyōnō District|ja|巨濃郡}}, {{ill|Hōmi District|ja|法美郡}}, {{ill|Yakami District (Tottori)|ja|八上郡 (鳥取県)|lt=Yakami District|lt_ja=八上郡}}, {{ill|Ōmi District|ja|邑美郡}}, {{ill|Takakusa District|ja|高草郡}}, and {{ill|Keta District|ja|気多郡}} in {{ill|Tottori Prefecture|ja|鳥取県}}.

== Tutelary Shrine ==
'''{{ill|Ube Shrine|ja|宇倍神社}}''', the {{ill|ichi-no-miya|ja|一宮}} of {{ill|Inaba Province|ja|因幡国}} located in {{ill|Kokufu, Tottori|ja|国府町 (鳥取市)|lt=Kokufu-chō|lt_ja=国府町}}, {{ill|Tottori, Tottori|ja|鳥取市|lt=Tottori City|lt_ja=鳥取市}}. The enshrined deity is presently said to be {{ill|Takenouchi-no-Sukune|ja|武内宿禰}}, but the ancestor of the Inaba no Kuni no Miyatsuko is also considered to be the original enshrined deity{{要出典|date=2022年3月}}.

=== Tombs ===
{{empty section|date=2022年1月}}

== People ==
{{empty section|date=2022年1月}}

== Footnotes ==
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist}}

== See also ==
*{{ill|San'in|ja|山陰}}
*{{ill|Kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:いなはのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]]
[[Category:因幡国]]
[[Category:因幡国の氏族]]
[[Category:物部氏|-いなはのくにのみやつこ]]
[[Category:鳥取県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:稲葉国造]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
